name: Elm
description: A purely functional language for reliable web applications with no runtime exceptions

description.value: A purely functional language for reliable web applications with no runtime exceptions

initial-release.comment: Created by Evan Czaplicki as his thesis project; repository created April 19, 2012

latest-release.comment: Version 0.19.1 released October 21, 2019; the language has been stable since then

active-maintenance.comment: Last compiler release was in 2019; the language is stable but not actively developed. Community frameworks like Lamdera, Elm Land, and elm-pages continue ecosystem development

reactivity-model.comment: Uses a custom virtual DOM implementation optimized for immutable data structures

component-model.comment: Pure functions for views; The Elm Architecture (TEA) provides Model-Update-View pattern

rendering-paradigm.comment: Fully declarative with pure functions generating virtual DOM

state-management-approach.comment: The Elm Architecture provides a built-in, enforced state management pattern

opinionatedness.comment: Very opinionated: single architecture pattern (TEA), no escape hatches, compiler-enforced patterns

backward-compatibility.comment: Strong commitment to stability; 0.19.1 has been stable since 2019 with no breaking changes

primary-language.comment: Elm is its own functional programming language, not JavaScript

typescript-support.comment: Elm has its own type system; TypeScript is not applicable

template-syntax.comment: Views are pure Elm functions using the Html module; no templates or JSX

styling-approach.comment: External CSS or inline styles via Html.Attributes.style; libraries like elm-css available

build-required.comment: Elm compiler required to compile .elm files to JavaScript

reactive-primitives.comment: No signals or refs; state is managed through The Elm Architecture's Model type

computed-values.comment: Derived values via pure functions; computed in the view function from Model

two-way-binding.comment: No two-way binding; unidirectional data flow is enforced

event-handling.comment: Events via Html.Events module (onClick, onInput, etc.) that dispatch messages

conditional-rendering.comment: Via if/then/else expressions and pattern matching in view functions

list-rendering.comment: Via List.map and similar functions to generate Html elements

component-slots.comment: Functions can accept Html as arguments; no special slot syntax needed

teleport-portal.comment: No built-in portal; Elm controls its own DOM tree

suspense.comment: No Suspense; async operations handled via Commands and update function

error-boundaries.comment: Not needed; the compiler guarantees no runtime exceptions in Elm code

context-provide-inject.comment: No dependency injection; all data flows through the Model

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: No direct DOM access from Elm; by design, all DOM updates go through the virtual DOM

official-router.comment: elm/browser provides Browser.application for SPA routing

router-name.comment: Browser.application handles navigation; elm/url parses URLs

nested-routes.comment: Via URL parsing with elm/url; community packages like elm-spa provide higher-level routing

route-guards.comment: Implemented in update function; can check auth state before allowing navigation

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Elm compiles to a single bundle; code splitting requires external tools

scroll-restoration.comment: Not built-in; can be implemented via ports to JavaScript

built-in-state.comment: The Elm Architecture provides built-in Model for all state

built-in-global-state.comment: Single Model for entire app state; enforced by architecture

global-state-library.comment: TEA (Model-Update-View) is the built-in state management pattern

devtools-state-inspection.comment: elm-debugger provides state inspection in development mode

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; available via ports to localStorage

time-travel-debugging.comment: Built-in time-travel debugger ships with Elm; can replay all messages

official-cli.comment: elm CLI for compilation, elm reactor for dev server, elm init for project creation

hot-module-replacement.comment: elm-live and elm-watch provide HMR with state preservation

browser-devtools.comment: Built-in debugger accessible via elm make --debug

vs-code-extension.comment: Elmtooling.elm-ls-vscode provides LSP support, formatting, and diagnostics

error-messages.comment: Famous for exceptionally helpful error messages with suggestions and context

documentation-quality.comment: Excellent guide and API docs; interactive tutorial at Try Elm

bundle-size-core.comment: ~9 kB gzipped for TodoMVC example; no separate runtime, all compiled to JS

bundle-size-full.comment: ~29 kB gzipped for elm-realworld example; scales well with app complexity

virtual-dom.comment: Custom virtual DOM optimized for immutable data structures

compile-time-optimization.comment: Compiler performs optimizations; --optimize flag enables aggressive optimization

code-splitting.comment: Single bundle output by default; experimental code splitting available via external tools

lazy-components.comment: No lazy component loading; Html.Lazy provides lazy rendering within components

ssr-capable.comment: Via elm-pages, Lamdera, or running Elm output in Node.js

streaming-ssr.comment: Not built-in; elm-pages provides SSG, not streaming SSR

partial-hydration.comment: Full hydration; Elm controls entire app tree

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Elm is included in js-framework-benchmark but performs moderately; noted as memory hungry

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Elm is distributed via its own installer, not primarily through npm

component-libraries.comment: elm-ui, elm-css; smaller ecosystem due to Elm's unique language

meta-framework.comment: Several community meta-frameworks: elm-pages (SSG/SSR), Elm Land (file-based routing), Lamdera (full-stack)

major-companies-using.value: NoRedInk, Vendr, Microsoft, IBM, Prezi, CARFAX, Concourse CI
major-companies-using.comment: NoRedInk employs Elm creator Evan Czaplicki; Vendr is a billion-dollar company using Elm

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Evan Czaplicki employed by NoRedInk; community-driven development

discord-community-size.comment: Active Slack community (elmlang.slack.com) with thousands of members; Discord via Incremental Elm

job-market-demand.comment: Niche adoption; valued in healthcare, fintech, and mission-critical apps but few job postings

official-testing-library.comment: elm-explorations/test is the official testing package

testing-library-name.comment: elm-explorations/test for defining tests, elm-test CLI for running them

component-testing.comment: Via elm-explorations/test with unit and fuzz testing

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Works with Playwright, Cypress since output is standard JavaScript

snapshot-testing.comment: Available via elm-program-test and custom test runners

mobile-framework.comment: No official mobile solution; can be used in WebView-based apps

pwa-support.comment: No built-in PWA tooling; can be added manually with service workers

desktop-framework.comment: No official desktop solution; can use Electron or Tauri with Elm

learning-curve.comment: Requires learning functional programming concepts, new language syntax, and The Elm Architecture

concepts-to-learn.comment: Elm syntax, Types, Maybe/Result, The Elm Architecture, Commands, Subscriptions, Ports, JSON Decoders

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~10 minutes including Elm installation and understanding basic syntax

migration-path.comment: Complete rewrite required; no migration tools from JS frameworks

incremental-adoption.comment: Elm can target a single DOM node, enabling gradual adoption alongside existing JS
